unit.title: First Steps in English
unit.description: Greetings to Daily Life — 8 lessons covering the core of everyday English

lessons.title: Hello & goodbye
lessons.description: Learn everyday greetings and farewells used in English.

tasks.title: Meet the greetings
tasks.instruction: Read each greeting and its meaning.

cards.back: A formal or casual way to greet someone.
A casual way to say goodbye.
Said when leaving at night or before sleeping.
A warm way to say goodbye.

tasks.title: When do you say it?
tasks.instruction: Choose the correct greeting for the time shown.

questions.prompt: It is 9:00 AM. You meet your teacher. What do you say?
questions.explanation: 'Good morning' is used in the morning, before noon.

questions.prompt: It is 3:00 PM. You see your friend. What do you say?
questions.explanation: 'Good afternoon' is used between noon and 6 PM.

questions.prompt: You are leaving your friend's house at 9:00 PM. What do you say?
questions.explanation: 'Good night' is used as a farewell in the evening or before sleep.

questions.prompt: You meet someone at a party. Which is the most informal greeting?
questions.explanation: 'Hi' is the most casual and informal greeting.

tasks.title: Match the pairs
tasks.instruction: Match each greeting with the correct farewell.

tasks.title: Complete the greeting
tasks.instruction: Fill in the blank with the correct word.

questions.sentence: _____ you later! I have to go now.
Good _____, sir. Nice to meet you.

tasks.title: Watch a real conversation
tasks.instruction: Read this short dialogue carefully.

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Hi! Good morning!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Good morning! How are you?

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: I'm fine, thank you. And you?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm great, thanks! See you later!

dialogue.speaker: Ana
dialogue.line: Bye-bye! Take care!

tasks.title: Build the sentence
tasks.instruction: Arrange the words to make a correct greeting.

questions.note: A polite morning greeting to your teacher.
An evening greeting to a group.

tasks.title: True or false?
tasks.instruction: Decide if the statement is true or false.

questions.explanation: 'Good morning' is used before noon.
'Hi' is casual and friendly; 'Hello' is slightly more formal.

questions.statement: 'See you later' is a way to say goodbye.
questions.explanation: Yes, it is a common informal farewell.

tasks.title: What time is it?
tasks.instruction: Look at the clock. Choose the correct greeting.

tasks.title: Say it out loud
tasks.instruction: Read the situation and say the correct greeting aloud. Record your voice.
prompts: You arrive at school at 8 AM. Greet your teacher.
You meet a friend at 4 PM. Say hello informally.
You are leaving home at 10 PM. Say goodbye to your family.

tasks.title: Formal or informal?
tasks.instruction: Choose whether the situation calls for a FORMAL or INFORMAL greeting.

questions.prompt: You are meeting your company's CEO for the first time.
options: Good afternoon, sir. It's a pleasure to meet you.
questions.correct: Good afternoon, sir. It's a pleasure to meet you.
questions.explanation: Formal situations require formal language.

questions.prompt: You are texting your best friend.
options: Good morning. I hope this message finds you well.
Heyyy! What are you up to? 😄
questions.correct: Heyyy! What are you up to? 😄
questions.explanation: Casual texting with close friends is informal.

tasks.title: Finish the dialogue
tasks.instruction: Choose the best response to complete the conversation.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Hi there! How are you today?

dialogue.speaker: You
options: I'm doing well, thank you! How about you?
dialogue.correct: I'm doing well, thank you! How about you?
dialogue.explanation: The natural response to 'How are you?' is to answer and ask back.

tasks.title: Roleplay: First day at work
tasks.instruction: You are starting a new job. Greet your manager and two colleagues appropriately. Use at least 3 different greetings or farewells. Record or write your responses.
tasks.scenario: It is Monday morning, 9:00 AM. You walk into the office. Your manager, Sarah, is at the door. Your colleagues, Mike and Lily, are at their desks.

characters.name: Sarah (Manager)
characters.line: Good morning! You must be the new team member.

characters.name: Mike (Colleague)
characters.line: Hey! Welcome! Great to have you here.

tasks.user_task: Respond to each person with an appropriate greeting. At the end of the day (scenario skip), say goodbye to all three.

tasks.title: Write a short greeting message
tasks.instruction: Write a short message (3–5 sentences) introducing yourself to a new classmate. Use at least 2 greetings or farewell expressions.
tasks.example_output: Hi! My name is Priya. It's great to meet you. I hope we can be good friends. See you in class tomorrow!

lessons.title: Who am I? — Introducing yourself
lessons.description: Learn how to introduce yourself: name, age, nationality, and job.

tasks.title: Key vocabulary
tasks.instruction: Study these important words for introducing yourself.

cards.back: What you are called. 'My name is Ana.'
How old you are. 'I am 25 years old.'
The country you are from. 'I am Indian.'
What you do for work. 'I am a teacher.'
The city where you are from. 'I am from Mumbai.'
What you speak. 'I speak Hindi and English.'

tasks.title: Grammar: 'To be' — I am / My name is
tasks.instruction: Study the grammar pattern for introducing yourself.

content.note: Use 'am' only with 'I'. Use 'is' with he/she/it. Use 'are' with you/we/they.

tasks.title: Complete with am / is / are
tasks.instruction: Fill in the blank with am, is, or are.

tasks.title: Choose the correct sentence
tasks.instruction: Pick the grammatically correct introduction.

questions.prompt: How does Carlos introduce his name correctly?
questions.explanation: Use 'is' with 'my name'.

questions.prompt: How does Priya say her age correctly?
questions.explanation: Use 'I am + number + years old' to state your age.

tasks.title: A self-introduction
tasks.instruction: Read this dialogue. Notice how the person introduces themselves.

dialogue.speaker: Sara
dialogue.line: Hi! My name is Sara. I am from Brazil.

dialogue.speaker: James
dialogue.line: Nice to meet you, Sara! I am James. I am from the UK.

dialogue.speaker: Sara
dialogue.line: How old are you, James?

dialogue.speaker: James
dialogue.line: I am 24 years old. And you?

dialogue.speaker: Sara
dialogue.line: I am 22. I am a student. What do you do?

dialogue.speaker: James
dialogue.line: I am a teacher.

tasks.title: Build the introduction
tasks.instruction: Arrange the words into a correct sentence.

tasks.title: Match question and answer
tasks.instruction: Match each question with the correct answer.

tasks.title: Choose the right occupation
tasks.instruction: Select the correct job title based on the description.

questions.description: This person teaches students in a school.
This person cooks food in a restaurant.

tasks.title: Introduce yourself
tasks.instruction: Record a short self-introduction covering: name, age, country, and job.
tasks.prompt: Hello! My name is _____. I am _____ years old. I am from _____. I am a _____.

tasks.title: Fix the mistakes
tasks.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in each sentence.

tasks.title: Negative introductions
tasks.instruction: Rewrite the sentence in negative form.

content.note: To make 'to be' negative, add 'not': I am not, She is not, They are not.

tasks.title: Write your introduction
tasks.instruction: Write a paragraph of 5–7 sentences introducing yourself. Include: name, age, nationality, job or studies, a hobby, and a language you speak.
tasks.example_output: Hi! My name is Meera. I am 26 years old. I am from Chennai, India. I am a software engineer. I love reading and cooking. I speak Tamil, Hindi, and English.

tasks.title: Roleplay: Speed networking event
tasks.instruction: You have 60 seconds to introduce yourself to a new contact at a professional event. Speak naturally and confidently.
tasks.scenario: You are at a professional networking event. A stranger approaches you.

characters.name: Stranger
characters.line: Hello! I don't think we've met. Who are you?

tasks.title: Read and answer
tasks.instruction: Read the introduction below and answer the questions.
tasks.passage: My name is Lena Müller. I am 31 years old. I am from Berlin, Germany, but I now live in London. I am a graphic designer. I work for a media company. I speak German and English, and I am learning Spanish. In my free time, I enjoy hiking and cooking.

questions.question: How many languages does Lena speak or is learning?

lessons.title: Asking about others
lessons.description: Learn how to ask questions about other people using question words.

cards.back: Asks about a thing. 'What is your name?'
Asks about a person. 'Who is she?'
Asks about a place. 'Where are you from?'
Asks about time. 'When is your birthday?'
Asks about age. 'How old are you?'
Asks about number. 'How many siblings do you have?'

questions.prompt: ___ is her name?
___ is he from?
___ old is your brother?
___ is your birthday?

tasks.title: Ask yes/no questions
tasks.instruction: Rewrite each statement as a yes/no question.

content.note: For yes/no questions: move 'am/is/are' before the subject.

questions.explanation: 'name' is singular, so use 'is'.
In questions, 'is' comes before the subject 'she'.
Use 'are' with 'they'.

tasks.title: Ask the question
tasks.instruction: Look at the answer. Ask the correct question using a question word.

tasks.title: Complete the interview
tasks.instruction: Fill in the questions in the interview.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: [Q1: Ask for her name]

dialogue.speaker: Applicant
dialogue.line: My name is Lin Wei.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: [Q2: Ask where she is from]

dialogue.speaker: Applicant
dialogue.line: I am from Shanghai, China.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: [Q3: Ask her job/role]

dialogue.speaker: Applicant
dialogue.line: I am a software developer.

tasks.title: Write 5 questions
tasks.instruction: Imagine you are interviewing a new student in your class. Write 5 questions you would ask them.
tasks.example_output: 1. What is your name? 2. Where are you from? 3. How old are you? 4. What do you study? 5. What are your hobbies?

tasks.title: Roleplay: Getting to know a new classmate
tasks.scenario: You are in your first English class. You meet a classmate you don't know. Ask them at least 5 different questions about themselves and respond when they ask you.

characters.name: Classmate
characters.line: Hi! I don't think we've spoken before. I'm really curious to know more about you!

checklist: Asked about their job or studies
Asked one additional question of your choice

lessons.title: People around me — family & friends
lessons.description: Learn family vocabulary and how to describe relationships using possessive pronouns.

cards.back: Your grandparent who is a woman.
Your grandparent who is a man.
A person you like and trust.

tasks.title: Grammar: Possessives — my, your, his, her, our, their

questions.prompt: That is Carlos. ___ sister is a doctor.
Sara and Tom are married. ___ son is 5 years old.
___ grandmother lives in the countryside.

tasks.title: Label the family tree
tasks.instruction: Look at the family tree and answer the relationship questions.

questions.context: Maria is Tom's mother. What is Tom to Maria?
Tom and Lisa are children of the same parents. What is Lisa to Tom?
Maria's father is still alive. What does Tom call Maria's father?

questions.sentence: I have one brother and one sister. We are ___.

questions.statement: Your aunt is your father's sister.
Your cousin is your grandparent's child.
questions.explanation: Your cousin is your uncle's or aunt's child.
'His' is used for males; 'her' is for females.

tasks.title: Talk about your family
tasks.instruction: Describe your family in 4–6 sentences. Mention who they are and one fact about each person.
tasks.prompt: I have a ___. My ___ is ___. My ___ works as a ___...

questions.explanation: Plural: brothers.
'parents' is plural, so use 'live'.

questions.sentence: I have a sister. Her name are Mia.
questions.explanation: 'name' is singular, use 'is'.

tasks.title: Read about a family
tasks.passage: My name is Ahmed. I have a small family. My mother's name is Fatima and my father's name is Hassan. I have one older sister named Nadia. She is 28 years old and she is a dentist. My father is a retired teacher and my mother is a homemaker. We also have a dog named Max. My best friend is called Omar. His family lives next door.

tasks.title: Describe your family
tasks.instruction: Write a paragraph about your family (real or imaginary). Include: number of people, names, relationships, and one fact about each person.

tasks.title: Roleplay: Show a family photo
tasks.scenario: You are showing a photo of your family to a new friend. Describe who everyone is and tell something interesting about each person.

characters.name: Friend
characters.line: Oh, is that a family photo? Tell me about everyone in it!

checklist: Used at least 5 family vocabulary words
Used possessives correctly (my, his, her, their)
Gave at least one fact about each person mentioned

lessons.title: Where I live — places & locations
lessons.description: Learn vocabulary for places in a town and prepositions of place.

cards.back: A home in a building with many units.
A large shop selling food and goods.
An outdoor green area for relaxing.
Where you keep or get money.
A place to eat cooked meals.
A place with books you can read or borrow.
Where you wait for the bus.
A path for vehicles and people.

prepositions.example: The cat is in the house.
The book is on the table.
I am at the bus stop.
The bank is next to the park.

prepositions.meaning: in the middle of two things
prepositions.example: The library is between the bank and the hospital.

questions.prompt: The supermarket is ___ the park.
I wait for the bus ___ the bus stop.
She lives ___ a small apartment.

tasks.title: Match the place to its purpose

questions.sentence: The school is ___ the hospital and the park.
I live ___ a flat in the city.
There is a café ___ to the library.
The bus stop is ___ the supermarket.

tasks.title: Read the map
tasks.instruction: Look at the description and choose the correct place.

questions.context: It is next to the park, and between the bank and the restaurant.
It is on Main Street, across from the park.

questions.statement: 'In' is used for a specific point, like a bus stop.
questions.explanation: 'At' is used for specific points. 'In' is for enclosed spaces.

questions.statement: 'Between' means in the middle of two things.
You say: 'I live at a house.'
questions.explanation: Correct: 'I live in a house.'

tasks.title: Describe your neighbourhood
tasks.instruction: Describe where you live using at least 4 place words and 3 prepositions.
prompts: What places are near your home?
What is next to or between places near you?

questions.correct: The school is near the park
The bank is between the library and the restaurant

questions.sentence: I am in the bus stop.
questions.correction: I am at the bus stop.

questions.sentence: She lives at a big house.
questions.correction: She lives in a big house.

questions.sentence: The café is next the bank.
questions.correction: The café is next to the bank.

tasks.title: Describe your ideal neighbourhood
tasks.instruction: Write 5–7 sentences describing your ideal neighbourhood. Use place vocabulary and prepositions.

tasks.title: Give directions
tasks.scenario: A tourist stops you on the street and asks for help finding the library. Use prepositions and place words to give clear directions.

characters.name: Tourist
characters.line: Excuse me! Could you help me? I'm looking for the library. I'm new here.

tasks.title: Read: A description of a town
tasks.passage: I live in a small but lively town called Greenfield. My house is on Oak Street, near the central park. There is a supermarket next to my house, which is very convenient. The library is between the town hall and the bank, just five minutes on foot. There is also a good hospital at the north end of the town. I love living here because everything is close.

questions.question: What street does the writer live on?
What is between the town hall and the bank?
Where is the hospital?
questions.answer: At the north end of the town.

questions.question: Why does the writer like the town?

lessons.title: My day — daily routines
lessons.description: Learn to talk about your daily routine using time expressions and the simple present tense.

cards.back: Stop sleeping and open your eyes.
Clean your teeth with a toothbrush.
Eat the first meal of the day.
Leave home to get to work or school.

tasks.title: Grammar: Simple present tense for routines

examples: She brushes her teeth every morning.
He goes to work at 9 AM.
We watch TV in the evening.
content.note: Add -s or -es to the verb when the subject is he, she, or it.

tasks.title: Put the routine in order
tasks.instruction: Put the daily activities in a logical order from morning to night.

questions.prompt: She ___ up at 6 AM every day.
We ___ dinner at 7 PM.
He ___ to school by bus.

questions.sentence: I wake up ___ 7 o'clock in the morning.
He usually ___ TV in the evening.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: Maria wakes up at 6:30 AM every day.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: She brushes her teeth and has breakfast at 7 AM.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: She goes to work at 8 AM by train.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: She has lunch at 1 PM with her colleagues.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: She comes home at 6 PM and cooks dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: She watches TV for an hour and goes to bed at 10 PM.

tasks.title: About Maria's routine
tasks.instruction: Based on the story above, is the statement true or false?

questions.explanation: She wakes up at 6:30 AM.

questions.statement: She goes to work by train.
She goes to bed at 11 PM.
questions.explanation: She goes to bed at 10 PM.
Add -s for he/she/it.
Adverbs of frequency go before the main verb but after 'to be'.
Use 'have' with we/I/you/they.

tasks.title: Describe your daily routine
tasks.instruction: Talk about your typical weekday from morning to night. Use at least 6 routine verbs and 3 time expressions.

tasks.title: Write about your routine
tasks.instruction: Write a paragraph about your daily routine (6–8 sentences). Include times and frequency adverbs.

tasks.title: Roleplay: My new routine
tasks.scenario: You have just moved to a new city and started a new job. A friend calls to ask what your new daily life is like.

characters.name: Friend
characters.line: Hey! So, what's your new routine like? I'm so curious — how's the new job and new city?

checklist: Used he/she/it -s verb form at least once (while describing a colleague etc.)
Used at least 3 frequency adverbs (always, usually, sometimes, never)
Mentioned morning, afternoon, and evening activities

tasks.title: Read and compare routines
tasks.passage: Jake is a student. He usually wakes up at 8 AM. He never eats breakfast — he just drinks coffee. He goes to university at 9 AM. He has lunch at 2 PM and sometimes does sport in the afternoon. He always studies in the evening and goes to bed at midnight. His flatmate, Leo, is different. Leo wakes up at 6 AM and always has a big breakfast. He goes to work at 7:30 AM and comes home at 5 PM. Leo always goes to bed before 10 PM.

questions.question: What does Jake drink instead of eating breakfast?
What is different about Leo's morning routine compared to Jake's?
questions.answer: Leo wakes up earlier and always has breakfast; Jake doesn't eat breakfast.

questions.question: Who goes to bed later — Jake or Leo?

lessons.title: What I like — hobbies & preferences
lessons.description: Learn to talk about hobbies, likes, and dislikes using 'like', 'love', 'hate', and 'enjoy'.

cards.back: Viewing films at home or cinema.
Enjoying music through headphones or speakers.

examples: We don't like getting up early.

questions.prompt: She loves ___ in the sea.
questions.explanation: After 'love/like/hate/enjoy', use verb+ing.

questions.prompt: I don't like ___ up early.
questions.explanation: After 'don't like', use verb+ing.

dialogue.speaker: Ali
dialogue.line: What do you like doing in your free time?

dialogue.speaker: Sophie
dialogue.line: I love reading and cooking. What about you?

dialogue.speaker: Ali
dialogue.line: I enjoy playing football and listening to music. Do you like sport?

dialogue.speaker: Sophie
dialogue.line: I don't really like sport, but I don't mind cycling.

dialogue.speaker: Ali
dialogue.line: That's cool! I hate cycling — it's too tiring!

questions.explanation: She doesn't mind cycling — neutral, not hate.

tasks.title: Talk about your hobbies
tasks.instruction: Answer these questions about your hobbies. Record your response.
prompts: What do you love doing in your free time?
Is there something you don't mind doing but don't love?

questions.explanation: Use 'doesn't' for he/she/it.

tasks.title: Describe a hobby in detail
tasks.instruction: Choose one hobby you love. Write 4–6 sentences explaining: what it is, when you do it, why you love it, and who you do it with.

tasks.title: Read: My hobbies
tasks.passage: My name is Pablo. I have lots of hobbies. I love cooking — I especially enjoy making Spanish food. I like watching films on weekends, but I don't like horror movies. I also enjoy running in the mornings. It helps me feel energetic. My sister and I both like gardening. We grow vegetables and flowers. My brother hates gardening — he prefers gaming. What do you like doing?

questions.question: What type of cooking does Pablo enjoy?
What type of movies doesn't Pablo like?
What do Pablo and his sister grow?

tasks.title: Roleplay: Finding a hobby partner
tasks.scenario: You join an online community for people with shared interests. You are chatting with a new member to see if you have hobbies in common.

characters.name: NewMember
characters.line: Hi! I'm looking for someone to share hobbies with. What are you into?

checklist: Used love/like/enjoy/hate/don't mind at least once each
Asked the other person about their hobbies

tasks.title: Write a hobby profile
tasks.instruction: Write a short profile (80–120 words) for a hobby-sharing app. Include: hobbies you love, hobbies you like, one thing you don't mind, and one hobby you hate. Explain why for each.

lessons.title: Unit 1 review — putting it all together
lessons.description: A comprehensive review and practice of all Unit 1 topics.

tasks.title: Mixed vocabulary quiz
tasks.instruction: Choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: You meet your boss at 9 AM. What do you say?
My father's sister is my ___.
The hospital is ___ the bank and the library.
She ___ up at 7 AM every day.
I love ___ Italian food.

questions.sentence: My name ___ Kenji. I ___ from Japan.
I live ___ a small flat near the park.

tasks.title: Find all the mistakes
tasks.instruction: Each sentence has one error. Find and correct it.

questions.sentence: Good morning! I am name Carlos.
questions.correction: Good morning! My name is Carlos.

questions.sentence: He don't like waking up early.
questions.correction: He doesn't like waking up early.

questions.sentence: The café is next the bank.
questions.correction: The café is next to the bank.

questions.sentence: What is your age? I am have 25 years.

tasks.title: Read: A day in the life
tasks.passage: Hello! My name is Yuki. I am 24 years old and I am from Tokyo, Japan. I live in a small apartment near the train station. My flat is between a supermarket and a park, which is very convenient. I am a graphic designer. I usually wake up at 7:30 AM, have breakfast, and go to work at 9 AM. I always have lunch with my colleague, Hana. In the evenings, I like cooking and watching films. I love Japanese food, but I also enjoy making Italian pasta. My hobby is photography — I love taking pictures of the city. On weekends, I usually go cycling with my brother and sometimes visit my grandparents.

questions.question: Where does Yuki live?
questions.answer: In a small apartment near the train station in Tokyo.

questions.question: What are Yuki's two cooking interests?
What does Yuki do on weekends?
questions.answer: Goes cycling with her brother and visits her grandparents.

tasks.title: Write your own 'Day in the Life'
tasks.instruction: Write a paragraph like Yuki's (100–130 words) about yourself, covering: greeting + name + age + nationality, your home and its location, your job or studies, your daily routine, and your hobbies.

tasks.title: Unit 1 speaking summary
tasks.instruction: Record a 2-minute talk covering all Unit 1 topics: introduce yourself, describe your family, your home location, your daily routine, and your hobbies.

tasks.title: Final roleplay: Full conversation
tasks.scenario: You meet a new student from another country at your English language school. Have a full natural conversation covering all Unit 1 topics.

characters.name: NewStudent
characters.line: Hi! I just joined this class today. I don't know anyone yet. Is it OK if we chat for a bit?

checklist: Asked at least 4 questions about the other person
Discussed hobbies — both yours and asked about theirs

tasks.title: Unit 1 final quiz
tasks.instruction: Answer all 10 questions to complete Unit 1.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: introducing yourself.
Which is a farewell expression?
My mother's father is my ___.
___ is she from? — She is from Brazil.
I live ___ an apartment near the school.
He ___ up at 6 AM every day.
She always ___ (have) breakfast before work.
I love ___ in the mountains.
Correct the sentence: 'She don't like cooking.'
The bank is ___ the library and the park.

tasks.title: Unit 1 complete!
tasks.message: Congratulations! You have completed Unit 1 — First Steps in English. You can now greet people, introduce yourself, talk about your family, describe where you live, explain your daily routine, and discuss hobbies. You're ready for Unit 2!
